rag_rules: Fix unresolved references by using only visible identifiers from the current component scope; do not introduce new declarations.
Ensure type consistency in AGREE expressions: numeric variables must have numeric expressions, Boolean variables must have Boolean expressions.
Do not place implementation-level bindings or subcomponent paths in component type annexes; move valid assignments to implementation scope.
Remove or rewrite AGREE clauses that reference invalid or opaque data types, especially when they involve Boolean-to-integer conversions.
generic_rules: When generating AGREE contracts, always validate that referenced identifiers exist in the current scope.
Maintain type safety by ensuring all expressions match the expected data types of their targets.
Use only visible AADL features or declared helpers in AGREE annexes to avoid resolution errors.
Avoid placing implementation-specific bindings in component type contracts; use implementation annexes instead.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Constants and equations in AGREE should use scalar types: int, real, bool.
Guarantees in component type annexes should relate visible inputs and outputs, not internal subcomponents.
Assignments in implementation scope must target valid output features or declared auxiliary variables.
anti_patterns: Do not assume that unresolved identifiers can be resolved by adding new declarations; instead, remove or revise the clause.
Do not mix implementation-level references with component type contracts; this causes scope resolution errors.
Do not convert Boolean ports into integer expressions unless the AADL model explicitly supports such a mapping.
Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers unless they are present in the model's configured dependencies.
irrelevant_topics: type scope range assumption and guarantee
